commit.message:
fix(huly-os): WebSocket TX + collection fields for UI visibility (#102)

HulyAdapter now sends TX over WebSocket (same protocol as Huly
frontend) instead of REST-only. This enters the LiveQuery broadcast
pipeline so issues appear in the UI immediately.

Root cause: Issues created via REST TX API were missing three critical
collection fields (attachedTo, attachedToClass, collection) that
Huly's LiveQuery subscription uses to filter the issue list query.

Changes:
- WebSocket TX primary, REST fallback in HulyAdapter.sendTx()
- connectWebSocket() with hello handshake (JSON mode)
- Collection fields on all issue creates (attachedTo, attachedToClass,
  collection, proper kind string, lexorank, comments)
- Node 22 Blob→text conversion for WS message handling
- disconnect() method on IHulyAdapter interface
- ws-tx-test.ts: standalone WebSocket TX verification script
- backfill-collection-fields.ts: fixes 140 existing issues
- backfill-issue-fields.ts: earlier status/number/identifier backfill

Verified: 134 UT issues + GAME-8 now visible in Huly UI (Playwright)

[SPRINT-HULY-REST-LIVEQUERY-BRIDGE-017]

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Both advisories are high-severity DoS vulnerabilities in multer <=2.0.2,
pulled transitively via @nestjs/platform-express@11.1.14. npm override
forces resolution to 2.1.0. 0 audit vulnerabilities remaining.

fix: queue system audit — 10 fixes across indexer, client, contracts

Critical:
- eventFeed scan logic: lastScannedBlock set after scan, not before check
- reportSpawned moved to MapContext (after TX confirms), not WaitingRoom (before TX)
- Cleanup cron always queries MUD tables for ground-truth player count
- Queue position only counts 'waiting' entries, not 'ready'

Important:
- markSpawned restricted to 'ready' status only
- CAPTCHA expiry shows visible "expired" message
- Invite code unique constraint (creator_wallet, milestone) prevents race condition
- Polling continues in 'ready' state; detects slot expiry back to 'waiting'
- MaxPlayers off-by-one: > changed to >= (was allowing maxPlayers+1)
- AdminSystem: added adminSetPlayerCount for counter correction after upgrades

Polish:
- Auto-redirect to game board when isSpawned
- Input validation on all queue API endpoints (wallet format, invite code format)

fix: use dedicated /opt/nvidia-vulkan dir instead of /usr/lib64 for container mount

Mounting all of /usr/lib64 from the host (Amazon Linux 2) into Ubuntu
containers caused glibc version conflicts, crashing bash immediately.
Fix: use /opt/nvidia-vulkan containing only the Vulkan producer lib.

Updated launch template to v4 with matching UserData.

commit.message:
fix(fae): speech pipeline hardening — reliability, quality, observability (v0.8.84)

Phase A — Reliability:
- Bounded speech-segment queue decouples capture/VAD from STT/LLM/TTS processing
- Centralized speaking state via markAssistantSpeechStarted/Ended (eliminates split-brain)
- TTS watchdog: 30s per-synthesis timeout (task group) + 60s global stuck-speaking guard
- TTFA metric + per-turn ID tracing (phase1.ttfa_ms, phase1.audio_backpressure_drop_count)

Phase B — Quality:
- AVAudioConverter-based resampling replaces linear interpolation (reduces slur/smear)
- Refined looksLikeNonProse gate: explicit tool markers, JSON envelope detection, tightened heuristics
- Tool-call filler speech: spoken acknowledgement before tool execution gaps
- 12Hz TTS codec runtime confirmation log

Additional:
- EchoSuppressor shortUtteranceGuardMs reduced 6000→4000ms (was dropping real short utterances)
- Voice identity fae_self window tightened 10s→5s (closer to actual echo tail)
- 4 new tests for non-prose detection (400/400 passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add scripts/_adaptive.sh helper for lagging-goal task weighting

Introduces adaptive task weighting that boosts priority of pending tasks
aligned with lagging (unchecked) mission goals. Key functions:

- _adaptive_goal_keywords: extract keywords from mission Goals section
- _adaptive_lagging_keywords: extract keywords from unchecked Success Criteria
- _adaptive_task_matches: check if task title/tag aligns with lagging goals
- _adaptive_reweight_pending: batch-update pending task priorities with boost
- _adaptive_order_clause: SQL ORDER BY fragment for adaptive-weighted claiming
- _adaptive_status: diagnostic output for debugging

Mirrors the TypeScript extractMissionGoalKeywords() logic in bash,
configurable via SKYNET_ADAPTIVE_BOOST (default: 5).

commit.message:
display,mondata: cursor box-shadow fix and null ptr guard

- cursor: switch from border-bottom to box-shadow: inset so the altar
  glyph (_) remains visible during both blink phases; border-bottom
  affected inline element layout height causing interaction with the

fix: resolve FormatId AttributeError crashing Add/Edit Product pages (#1079)

* fix: use direct width/height access in Format.get_primary_dimensions()

The adcp library's FormatId class does not have a get_dimensions()
method - that only exists on our custom FormatId subclass. Since
Format inherits format_id typed as LibraryFormatId, calling
get_dimensions() crashes with AttributeError.

Access width/height fields directly instead, which exist on both
the library and custom FormatId classes.

Fixes #1077

<coworker>/<description> (e.g. "lexington/fix-auth"). All branch-to-coworker
resolution now goes through the worktree_branch_owners map exclusively.

  a single function that always requires the branch_owners map
- Remove legacy prefix-matching path that scanned COWORKER_NAMES

commit.message:
fix(scraper): compute monthly production as kWh/panel/month × zone panel count

- Replace kWh/day × DAYS_PER_MONTH formula with kWh/panel/month × AllZones[i].TotalSolarPanel
- Parse editor.AllZones to get per-zone panel counts before summing projections
- Zones with 0 panels contribute 0 kWh (multiplied out implicitly)
- Add single-zone fallback using root TotalSolarPanel when AllZones is absent
- Log zone panel counts for debuggability
- Remove DAYS_PER_MONTH usage from this block (constant retained for potential future use)
- Fixes 5.87x annual production overcount (e.g. 45,710 → 7,782 kWh for 13-panel Zone B)

commit.message:
refactor(auth): rely only on better-auth logic (#382)

* refactor(auth): avoid save accounta data in better-auth

* refactor(auth): remove custom oidc token cookie/crypto/refresh layer

Delete cookie.ts, token.ts, crypto.ts — the custom stateless token
management layer built before Better Auth supported storeAccountCookie.

Better Auth 1.4+ handles all of this natively via storeAccountCookie and
auth.api.getAccessToken(), so the custom layer is no longer needed.

- Remove OidcTokenData and TokenResponse from types.ts (no longer stored
  in custom cookies)
- Remove getOidcProviderAccessToken, getOidcIdToken, saveAccountTokenToCookie,
  databaseHooks, and cookie imports from auth.ts
- Remove getOidcIdToken and saveAccountToken (cookie helpers) from utils.ts
- Reduce proxy.ts to a passthrough (token refresh now handled by Better Auth)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(auth): use auth.api.getAccessToken for token retrieval

Replace the custom getValidOidcToken() and getOidcIdToken() calls with
Better Auth's built-in auth.api.getAccessToken(), which handles token
reading, automatic refresh, and idToken retrieval natively.

- api-client.ts: call auth.api.getAccessToken() instead of getValidOidcToken()
- actions.ts: remove clearOidcTokenAction (Better Auth signOut() clears
  account_data automatically); use auth.api.getAccessToken() to get idToken
  for RP-Initiated Logout
- auth-client.ts: remove clearOidcTokenAction call from signOut()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update test suite for removed auth token layer

- Delete tests for removed modules: auth-cookie-mode, auth (db token tests),
  token, cookie-proxy, crypto
- Update auth-client.test.ts: remove clearOidcTokenAction mock and assertions
- Update utils.test.ts: remove cookie-based getOidcIdToken / saveAccountToken tests
- Update vitest.setup.ts: add auth.api.getAccessToken global mock returning
  "mock-test-token"; remove @/lib/auth/token mock (module deleted)
- Update catalog/actions.test.ts: fix comment to reference getAccessToken

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(auth): add unit tests for getOidcSignOutUrl

* refactor(auth): delete proxy.ts (dead code)

proxy.ts was the pre-SSR token refresh middleware. Now that Better Auth
handles refresh natively via auth.api.getAccessToken(), the file was
reduced to a NextResponse.next() passthrough.

Next.js only executes middleware from middleware.ts — nothing imported or
invoked proxy.ts, making it a no-op. Remove it entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mocks): correct createdAt placeholder in registries fixture

Was "id" (copy-paste artifact), should be a valid ISO 8601 timestamp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

design: 히어로 레이아웃 전면 개편 + 인터랙션 강화

- 히어로: 2컬럼 레이아웃 (텍스트 좌 + 스탯 카드 우 스태거드)
- 슬로건: 'Fine'·'Find' 각각 인디고 포인트 처리
- 배경: dot-grid 패턴 + 인디고 글로우 블러 효과
- 마퀴 티커: 예술 카테고리 무한 스크롤 바 추가
- 기능 소개: 에디토리얼 리스트 스타일 (호버 슬라이드 + 태그)
